summary: Read-only audit of DinoBrain auto GitHub push for the app/data loop. Evidence found: hook and finish_task auto-sync code paths exist and are scoped by allowed_paths; git_sync is dry-run only; data Git hooks are installed and verified; the synthetic closed-loop verifier passed with hook, finish_task, reviewed-growth, and trace_only assertions. Blocker: an actual live diagnostic hook auto_sync created local data commit c9a593d but push failed, leaving dinobrain-data ahead of origin/main by one commit. Current data sync dry-run shows remaining uncommitted data changes require manual review/commit; app repo also has uncommitted installer hook merge changes not pushed.
decisions: Treat hook auto-sync as implemented but not reliable-live until push failure handling is fixed or a trusted live prompt shows pushed=true against the real data remote.
Do not push or commit during this audit because the user requested read-only.
Keep git_sync as manual dry-run evidence; it does not commit or push by design.
next_steps: Fix or harden auto_sync push failure reporting/recovery so a failed push cannot silently leave a local data commit ahead of origin.
Manually review and decide whether to push local data commit c9a593d and the outstanding data repo changes.
Commit/push the app repo installer hook merge changes only after review and verification.
